channel.title: Blue Cross VT News Mentions
channel.description: Mentions of BCBSVT, Blue Cross VT, and Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont from Vermont news outlets.

item.title: MyChamplainValley: Northern Border Regional Commission grants announced for Vermont - water, transportation work in Newport
item.description:
Source: MyChamplainValley

Matched terms: Health care

... nounced for Vermont - water, transportation work in Newport The recipients of $7.62 million in funding for economic development in Vermont have been announced, including water and healthcare improvements. The recipients of $7.62 million in funding for economic development in Vermont have been announced, including water and healthcare improvements.

Read the original story

item.title: KFF Health News: California Health Worker Union, Hospital Association Tout Dueling Ballot Initiatives
item.description:
Source: KFF Health News

Matched terms: Hospitals, Hospital & nurse labor

California Health Worker Union, Hospital Association Tout Dueling Ballot Initiatives Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

Read the original story

item.title: Vermont Business Magazine: Vermont gets $7.62 million in Northern Border Regional Commission grants
item.description:
Source: Vermont Business Magazine

Matched terms: Health care

Vermont is receiving $7.62 million in Northern Border Regional Commission grants to support projects including modernizing healthcare facilities.

Why included: Grant funding for healthcare facilities in Vermont.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News National Health Policy Search: Surprising ways menopause can affect your mouth - The Washington Post
item.source: Google News National Health Policy Search
item.description:
Source: Google News National Health Policy Search

Matched terms: Women's health

Read the original story

item.title: Healthcare Dive: Major Medicare Advantage insurers appear to deny care for profit, federal watchdog finds
item.description:
Source: Healthcare Dive

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare, Health insurance, Health care

A federal watchdog report indicates that major Medicare Advantage insurers may be denying care for profit.

Why included: Medicare Advantage insurer practices and potential profit motive.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger Facebook: VTDigger Facebook post: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’
item.description:
Source: VTDigger Facebook

Matched terms: Blue Cross, Health care

BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer cheaper plans, but the state's healthcare regulator doubts they will yield real savings.

Why included: Names BCBSVT directly, regulatory action on coverage

Comments:

  • Janet Fitzpatrick: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable.
  • Mark Byland: sp/healthcare/healthprofit
  • Kristin Bryn Reid: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you
  • Dylan Greer: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare
  • Janice Wright: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors
  • Jay Files: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything
  • Amy Beth: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous.
  • Ruth Miller: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis.
  • Beth Stevens: My plan for myself a child was supposed to be 650 a month with a 5k deductible, got my bill over 900 a month. I canceled as I was still paying for meds and co pay
  • Matthew Funk: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: ADA urges dental-specific approach in CMS interoperability, prior authorization proposal - American Dental Association
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Prior authorization & claims, Health records & interoperability

The American Dental Association is advocating for a dental-specific approach to CMS interoperability and prior authorization proposals.

Why included: Prior authorization and interoperability policy for dental care.

Read the original story

item.title: Healthcare Dive: CMS increases oversight of state Medicaid demonstrations
item.description:
Source: Healthcare Dive

Matched terms: Medicaid

CMS increases oversight of state Medicaid demonstrations States will have to provide more rigorous financial analyses for their Medicaid demonstrations beginning in 2027, according to new federal guidance.

Read the original story

item.title: The Hill Health Care: Democrats seek next ObamaCare ahead of midterms amid rising health costs
item.description:
Source: The Hill Health Care

Matched terms: ACA & marketplace, Health care, Medical costs & billing

Democrats seek next ObamaCare ahead of midterms amid rising health costs There is growing momentum within the Democratic Party to find its next ObamaCare heading into the midterms, as healthcare costs become more untenable and American anger over affordability persists. Healthcar ...

Read the original story

item.title: Google News National Health Policy Search: Final Rules for Medicaid Work Requirements Are Out. Here’s What You Need To Know. - KFF Health News
item.source: Google News National Health Policy Search
item.description:
Source: Google News National Health Policy Search

Matched terms: Medicaid

Final Rules for Medicaid Work Requirements Are Out. Here’s What You Need To Know. The Trump administration has laid out what millions of Americans on Medicaid must do to prove they’re working or completing other activities. Health policy researchers and consumer advocates say ...

Read the original story

item.title: KFF Health News: 1 in 4 Covered California Enrollees Could Get State Aid Under Newsom Proposal
item.description:
Source: KFF Health News

Matched terms: Health insurance, Premiums & rate review

1 in 4 Covered California Enrollees Could Get State Aid Under Newsom Proposal California is considering expanding financial help for low-income residents struggling to pay high health insurance premiums after losing federal subsidies. But relief for state marketplace customers will be limited. Here’s who may get help and what it could mean for premiums.

Read the original story

item.title: The Hill Health Care: Planned Parenthood affiliate sues Alaska over telehealth abortion ban
item.description:
Source: The Hill Health Care

Matched terms: Telehealth, Reproductive health

Planned Parenthood affiliate sues Alaska over telehealth abortion ban Planned Parenthood’s Northwest affiliate is suing Alaska to challenge the state’s ban on telehealth abortion. In a lawsuit filed Thursday in Alaska state court, Planned Parenthood Great Northwest, Hawaii, Alaska, Indiana, Kentucky said ...

item.title: Vermont Public: State regulators approve new surgery center for the Upper Valley
item.description:

Why included: New surgery center impacts hospital competition and costs.

item.title: Healthcare Dive: Health plans say AI is pushing healthcare costs higher
item.description:
Source: Healthcare Dive

Matched terms: Health insurance, Health care

Health plans say AI is pushing healthcare costs higher Nearly 70% of surveyed health plans said providers’ use of AI documentation and coding tools was a top three trend inflating commercial healthcare costs next year, according to a new report from PwC.

Read the original story
